title: Lifetimes 2
description: Not all parameters need to share the same lifetime. Sometimes only one input determines the output's lifetime.
instructions: Fix the lifetime annotations so the function compiles correctly.
hints: Since the function always returns x, only x's lifetime matters for the return.
The parameter y doesn't need the same lifetime as x and the return value.
This code should compile because result's lifetime is tied to string1, not string2.
explanation: When a function always returns a reference from a specific parameter, only that parameter needs to share the return value's lifetime. Here, `y` doesn't need lifetime `'a` because the return value is always `x`. This gives the compiler more flexibility and allows `string2` to be dropped while `result` is still valid.
